rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MICHAEL W. OBERSCHELP TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #14, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) When the trust was created, former section 3611 of the Probate Code did not list a non-special needs trust as one of the permissible options from which the court could have selected to order distribution of money obtained as a result of a minor's compromise. The petition admits that the subject trust was not set up as a special needs trust within the meaning of section 3604. Section 3611(g), which was added 3 years after the trust was created, gives an additional option to the court to authorize distribution of the minor's money to a non-special needs trust which must be revocable once the minor turns 18. 2) The trustee requests authority to spend $850,000 to purchase a home in Florida for the beneficiary and his parents. The trustee proposes that property taxes, insurance, HOA fees, and costs of maintenance and extraordinary repairs be paid from the trust, while the beneficiary's parents are to pay all other expenses including costs of ordinary repairs and utilities. The trustee also requests an extraordinary service fee equal to 2% of the gross sale price, plus $2,000 as an annual fee to hold the real property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MARSHALL DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Objections were just filed on 9/13/21; a courtesy copy has not yet been received by the assigned Examiner. Additionally, the following issues must be addressed: Proof of Service by First-Class Mail - Civil is NOT appropriate in Probate. The only correct form of notice for accountings in decedent's estates is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing (DE-120), on the reverse of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. The amendment, while file stamped 8/19/21, was not actually E-filed (available to view) until 8/24/21, so notice mailed before the pleading was available for viewing is defective. The amendment claims that all other schedules remain the same but Schedule G should have changed. The attorney costs in paragraph 3 of the amendment are not complete: the date, payee, description, and amount must be set forth for each cost. The filing fee is never $461.50 or $446.50 or $457.50, and E-filing fees are usually $5, not $11.50. Parking is not reimbursable. [LRSF 14.58 B.] Original bank statements must be filed to verify the ending balances. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Probate Code Section 9700 permits a personal representative to deposit money of the estate in an insured account in a financial institution in this state (as opposed to investing money in other forms); the permission does not apply to investing in any bank in any other state. This Court has no jurisdiction over assets held in another state. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF REBECCA KING MORROW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) In addition to the requirements of CRC 7.651, the petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.] If the requested compensation/fees and reserve are granted, it appears that there will be a negative amount of cash in the estate's account. 2) The executor sold the 120 shares of Wells Fargo stock but does not disclose for how much. Ordinarily, even on waiver of account, the sales of assets/changes in the form of assets are reported in more detail. [CRC 7.550(b).] 3) The proposed order shows a different balance of cash on hand as of 7/12/21. The executor claimed in her petition that the balance of cash on hand as of 7/12/21 was $49,897.95. [See paragraph 19, p. 8, of the petition.] The Court cannot find and order what has not been set forth in a verified pleading (or varies from the verified pleading). Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCISCO J. CLAUDIO AND VIRGINIA P. CLAUDIO TRUST **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY**
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A trust accounting cannot be approved until the assessment fee in the related conservatorship case bas been paid. No ex parte petition has been filed yet to increase bond (as also noted in a prior tentative ruling). [CRC 7.204.] The I&A filed on 4/27/21 shows that it is just a Partial No. 1, listing a portion of the estate. No Final I&A appears to have been filed yet. If a third party is hired and paid to prepare Court conformed accountings, such fee must be deducted from the fiduciary's fee if the fiduciary fee is based on 1% of the market value of the estate assets. "Frequently" does not adequately address the time spent in the performance of the trustees' duties [CRC 7.776(6)]. (Have the subtrusts not yet been funded?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/5/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. The alternate executor named in the will is missing from item 8 of the petition. The nominations by the heirs are not relevant, as the beneficiaries named in the will are the parties who now have standing to nominate an administrator with will annexed and/or waive bond for him. (The will waived bond only for the executor or alternate executor.) If no subscribing witness is available as a witness within the meaning of Section 240 of the Evidence Code, Probate Code Section 8221 provides for an alternative way for the court to permit proof of the will. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/2/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

[LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): Amended Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary [Filed 07/01/2021 by Tatiana Anatolievna Donskaya] 1) Objections were filed to the original filing. No objections filed to the Amended Petition. 2) Amended petition attached a copy of decedent's will without a certified copy translated into English. See LRSF 14.15 D. 3) Not a certified copy of the will. 4) 7/27/2021 Proof of publication does not include all the aka names of decedent used in the amended petition. Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary [Filed 08/02/2021 by Scott Fraser] 1) The petition does not have the name of Decedent in the caption. It appears an amended petition needs to be filed, re-published, and re-noticed with Decedent's name in the caption. 2) Not all documents attached to the petition appear to follow LRSF 14.15 D. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SCOTT KIN WAH CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) Notice of the amended petition was given before the amended petition was filed. See LRSF 14.14(A). New notice must be published and mailed. 2) The proposed personal representative's consent to serve must be filed. LRSF 14.24. 3) No competing petition has been filed by the objector. No will has been filed. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MAHLON FRAZER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] Courtesy copies of papers filed on 9/3/21 and later have not yet been received by the assigned Examiner, likely as a result of the Court clerks being extremely short-staffed. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 as is the alternate beneficiary, Project Open Hand. (Probate Code Section 8421, not 6421, provides for the person not named in the will to seek appointment as executor. A first cousin once removed (not a "second cousin") is not required to be listed in item 8 or to be given notice if his/her parent, who is the first cousin, survived the decedent.) The declaration filed on 9/3/21 presumably addresses why no paternal heirs are listed; if not, the petitioner must file a supplement to his petition addressing efforts to locate paternal heirs. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/1/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID L. DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Courtesy copies of papers filed on 9/7/21 and later have not yet been received by the assigned Examiner. Not all persons named in the will are listed in item 8 of the petition or appear to have been given notice. Also, the trustee of the devisee trust is missing from item 8 of the petition. If the petitioner is the trustee, then all of the beneficiaries of the trust must also be listed, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. [LRSF 14.18 C.2.] The petitioner is presumably the person having legal custody of the minors, however, item 8 of the petition does not address this. (Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).]) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
